tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows significant renal impairment (elevated creatinine, marked proteinuria), hypoglycemia, glucosuria, ketonuria, and low urine specific gravity, suggesting possible diabetic nephropathy or another cause of renal dysfunction with impaired glucose handling. Urine findings (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal) may indicate underlying urinary tract pathology. Further clinical correlation and nephrology evaluation are recommended.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus

text.div: Provider Organization: Azienda Sanitaria Locale n° 7 Musei
